title: Receiver and lidar system
abstract: A receiver (10), suitable for a LIDAR system (1), receives an input signal. The input signal is based on a detection of a superposition of a transmit signal emitted towards an object in a measurement configuration (30) and a reflected signal reflected by the object, the input signal being a periodically varying signal having a period T. The receiver (10) comprises a first filter to delay the input signal by a first retardation time to generate a first delayed signal. The receiver (10) further comprises a second filter to delay the first delayed signal by T/2 to generate a second delayed signal. The receiver (10) further comprises a frequency detector (18) to determine a frequency of an evaluation signal which is based on a difference of the first delayed signal and the second delayed signal. The measurement configuration (30) comprises a laser device that emits the transmit signal being triangularly frequency modulated. The LIDAR system (1) comprises a laser driving system (25) for driving the laser device and a receiver (10) to receive a detected current or voltage signal (37, 38) as an input signal, which signal further has a comparatively fast changing portion of small amplitude having the period T. The receiver 10 comprises the first filter and the second filter as components of a signal preconditioning system (41). A feedback signal may be input to a digital analog converter (44). A digital signal processor (43) may determine speed and distance of the object with improved accuracy.
claims:
Claims 2022PF00563 19 CLAIMS 1. A receiver (10), suitable for a LIDAR system (1), the receiver (10) being configured to receive an input signal (11), the input signal (11) being based on a detection of a superposition of a transmit signal (33) emitted towards an object (31) and a reflected signal (34) reflected by the object, the input signal (11) being a periodically varying signal having a period T, the receiver (10) comprising: a first filter (13) configured to delay the input signal by a first retardation time to generate a first delayed signal; a second filter (14) configured to delay the first delayed signal by T/2 to generate a second delayed signal, and a frequency detector (18) configured to determine a frequency of an evaluation signal which is based on a difference of the first delayed signal and the second delayed signal. 2. The receiver (10) according to claim 1, being configured to determine a distance and a speed of the object based on the frequency determined by the frequency detector (18). 3. The receiver (10) according to claim 1 or 2, further comprising an offset compensated amplifier (15) which determines the difference of the first delayed signal and the second delayed signal. 4. The receiver (10) according to any of the preceding claims, further comprising a duty cycle detector (19) configured to receive the evaluation signal, to analyze a duty cycle in dependence from time of the evaluation signal and to generate a duty cycle detector signal. 5. The receiver (10) according to claim 4, further comprising a control device (22) configured to receive the duty cycle detector signal and to determine a control signal for controlling the first filter (13). 

2022PF00563 20 6. The receiver (10) according to claim 5, wherein the frequency detector (18) further is configured to determine the frequency of the evaluation signal in dependence from time and to generate a frequency detector signal and the control device (22) is further configured to receive the frequency detector signal and to determine the control signal further based on the frequency detector signal. 7. The receiver (10) according to claim 4 or 5 wherein the control device (22) is further configured to output a driver control signal to a driving system (25) that modulates the transmit signal. 8. A LIDAR system (1) comprising: a laser device (32) configured to emit a transmit signal (33) towards an object (31), a wavelength of the transmit signal (33) being variable by varying a current injected in the laser device (32); a laser driving system (25) for driving the laser device (32); and a receiver (10) configured to receive an input signal (11), the input signal (11) being based on a superposition of the transmit signal (33) and a reflected signal (34) reflected by the object (31), the input signal (11) being a periodically varying signal having a period T, the receiver (10) comprising: a first filter (13) configured to delay the input signal (33) by a first retardation time to generate a first delayed signal; a second filter (14) configured to delay the first delayed signal by T/2 to generate a second delayed signal, and a frequency detector (18) configured to determine a frequency of an evaluation signal which is based on a difference of the first delayed signal and the second delayed signal. 9. The LIDAR system (1) according to claim 8, being configured to determine a distance and a speed of the object (31) based on the frequency determined by the frequency detector. 

2022PF00563 21 10. The LIDAR system (1) according to claim 8 or 9, further comprising an offset compensated amplifier (15) which determines the difference of the first delayed signal and the second delayed signal. 11. The LIDAR system (1) according to any of claims 8 to 10, further comprising a duty cycle detector (19) configured to receive the evaluation signal, to analyze a duty cycle in dependence from time of the evaluation signal and to generate a duty cycle detector signal. 12. The LIDAR system (1) according to claim 11, further comprising a control device (22) configured to receive the duty cycle detector signal and to determine a control signal for controlling the first filter (13). 13. The LIDAR system (1) according to claim 12, wherein the frequency detector (18) further is configured to determine the frequency of the evaluation signal in dependence from time and to generate a frequency detector signal and the control device (22) is further configured to receive the frequency detector signal and to determine the control signal further based on the frequency detector signal. 14. The LIDAR system (1) according to claim 13 or 14, wherein the control device (22) is further configured to output a driver control signal to the laser driving system (25), the laser driving system (25) is configured to generate a triangularly modulated current signal and a slope of the triangularly modulated current signal is controlled by the driver control signal. 15. An electronic device (5) comprising the LIDAR system (1) according to any of claims 8 to 14. 16. The electronic device (5) according to claim 15, being selected from a smartphone, a digital camera an autonomously driving unit, and wearables.
